skill.name: burp-suite-testing
skill.description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "intercept HTTP traffic", "modify web requests", "use Burp Suite for testing", "perform web vulnerability scanning", "test with Burp ...
skill.summary: Comprehensive Burp Suite guide for web application security testing, covering proxy interception, request modification, vulnerability scanning, and manual testing workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Burp Suite documentation skill for authorized web application security testing. Static findings (external_commands, filesystem, network) are FALSE POSITIVES - they represent example payloads shown in a reference section for educational purposes, not actual executable code. The skill contains proper operational boundaries and best practices. Risk level set to low due to educational nature of payload examples.

critical_findings.title: Example Payloads in Documentation
critical_findings.description: Static scanner detected command patterns (e.g., 'cat /etc/passwd', 'whoami') and path traversal examples (e.g., '../../../etc/passwd'). These are FALSE POSITIVES - example payloads shown in the 'Common Testing Payloads' reference section (SKILL.md:273-294) for educational purposes. They are markdown text examples, not executable code.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are clearly documented example payloads in a reference section, not actual code execution. The skill explains these are testing payloads for authorized security testing.

critical_findings.title: Misidentified Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
critical_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple lines (3, 198, 218, 234, 385), but no cryptographic code exists in this skill. This is a FALSE POSITIVE likely caused by the scanner misinterpreting documentation text.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: After reviewing the entire skill file, no cryptographic algorithms or implementations exist. The scanner likely misidentified references to security concepts as crypto code.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate Local Proxy Configuration
medium_findings.description: Network references to 127.0.0.1:8080 and http://burp are legitimate local proxy configurations for Burp Suite, not external network connections.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard local proxy settings for Burp Suite - 127.0.0.1 is localhost, and http://burp is the local certificate download endpoint.

low_findings.title: Educational Security Testing Content
low_findings.description: The skill provides comprehensive guidance on web application security testing using Burp Suite. All 'dangerous' patterns detected are example payloads in a reference section, which is standard practice for security training materials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is legitimate security testing documentation with proper operational boundaries, scope configuration guidance, and best practices for authorized testing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Example Penetration Testing Payloads
dangerous_patterns.description: Skill contains example payloads (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, command injection) in reference section. These are educational examples for authorized security testing, not malicious code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Standard security training documentation showing example payloads. Skill includes clear guidance on authorized testing only.

content.user_title: Test Web Applications with Burp Suite
content.value_statement: Burp Suite is a powerful web application security testing tool, but its comprehensive features can be overwhelming. This skill provides a structured workflow for intercepting HTTP traffic, identifying vulnerabilities, and conducting professional security assessments.
actual_capabilities: Intercept and modify HTTP requests and responses using Burp Proxy
Perform manual vulnerability testing with Repeater and Intruder tools
Configure target scope to focus testing on authorized applications
Execute automated vulnerability scans using Burp Scanner (Professional)
Analyze scan results and generate remediation reports
Test for common web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and business logic flaws
limitations: Automated scanner requires Burp Suite Professional (not available in Community Edition)
Cannot test applications that use certificate pinning without additional configuration
Community Edition has limited Intruder attack capabilities
Does not test client-side vulnerabilities in compiled binaries or mobile apps

use_cases.title: Security Assessment for New Web Application
use_cases.description: Conduct comprehensive security testing on a newly developed web application before production deployment to identify and remediate vulnerabilities.

use_cases.title: API Security Validation
use_cases.description: Test REST APIs for injection vulnerabilities, authentication bypasses, and improper access controls using request modification techniques.

use_cases.title: Vulnerability Research and Learning
use_cases.description: Learn web application security testing techniques by practicing with example vulnerabilities in controlled lab environments.
use_cases.target_user: Security students and aspiring penetration testers

prompt_templates.title: Basic HTTP Interception
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I intercept HTTP requests using Burp Suite? Walk me through configuring the proxy and capturing traffic from a web browser.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wanting to understand Burp Suite proxy setup

prompt_templates.title: Testing for SQL Injection
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use Burp Repeater to test a login form for SQL injection vulnerabilities. Include example payloads and how to interpret the responses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user testing authentication for SQL injection

prompt_templates.title: Business Logic Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Demonstrate how to test for business logic vulnerabilities in an e-commerce application using Burp Suite. How do I identify and exploit price manipulation issues?
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user focusing on business logic flaws

prompt_templates.title: Automated Vulnerability Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through running an automated vulnerability scan with Burp Suite Professional. How do I configure scan settings, monitor progress, and review findings?
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user wanting to run comprehensive automated scans

output_examples.input: How do I modify request parameters in Burp Suite?
output_examples.output: To modify request parameters: 1) Enable interception in Proxy > Intercept > Intercept is on, 2) Trigger the request in your browser, 3) Edit parameters directly in the intercepted request, 4) Click Forward to send the modified request. For repeated testing, right-click any request in HTTP history and select Send to Repeater for easy modification and resending.

output_examples.input: What are common SQL injection test payloads?
output_examples.output: Common SQL injection payloads include: ' OR '1'='1 (basic authentication bypass), ' OR '1'='1'-- (comment-based bypass), 1 UNION SELECT NULL-- (union-based injection), OR 1=1-- (numeric parameter injection). Always test with both GET and POST parameters, and monitor for error messages, different response times, or data disclosure.

best_practices: Always define and configure target scope before starting extensive testing to avoid accidentally testing unauthorized applications
Use Burp's embedded browser for reliable proxy integration without manual browser configuration
Save your Burp project regularly to preserve work and enable collaboration with team members
anti_patterns: Do not test applications without explicit authorization - unauthorized security testing is illegal
Avoid aggressive scanning that could cause denial of service - rate-limit automated scans
Never rely solely on automated scanner results - always manually verify findings to reduce false positives

faq.question: Is Burp Suite free?
faq.answer: Burp Suite offers both Community and Professional editions. Community Edition includes Proxy, Repeater, and basic Intruder functionality. Professional Edition adds automated Scanner, advanced Intruder features, and extension support.

faq.question: Can I test HTTPS websites with Burp Suite?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need to install Burp's CA certificate in your browser to intercept HTTPS traffic. Navigate to http://burp in your configured browser to download the certificate, then add it to your browser's trusted roots.

faq.question: What is the difference between Repeater and Intruder?
faq.answer: Repeater is for manual, ad-hoc testing of individual requests where you modify and resend one request at a time. Intruder automates repeated testing with multiple payloads, useful for fuzzing, brute force attacks, and parameter enumeration.

faq.question: How do I avoid testing out-of-scope websites?
faq.answer: Configure target scope by right-clicking your target host in Target > Site map and selecting 'Add to scope'. Then enable the display filter to 'Show only in-scope items' to exclude unwanted traffic from your view.

faq.question: Why is my browser not connecting through Burp proxy?
faq.answer: Verify the proxy listener is active in Proxy > Options, confirm browser proxy settings point to 127.0.0.1:8080, check for firewall blocking local connections, or use Burp's embedded browser for reliable setup.

faq.question: Is it legal to use Burp Suite for security testing?
faq.answer: Burp Suite is a legal tool, but you must only test applications you own or have explicit written authorization to test. Unauthorized penetration testing is illegal in most jurisdictions. Always document your authorization scope.
